evidence_quotes.quote: The videogame industry has reached a point where you pay real money for a videogame copy, but that videogame is online only, meaning you can't play it offline, or if your internet goes down for example, and not only that, the developers of said game, can, at any time shut down the servers of the game, making it completely unplayable.
a game that alot of videogame development companies have come to agree that you "rent", you do not buy it, and that is absurd.

paragraphs.text: I'm one of the people who voted for the stop killing games initiative. The videogame industry has reached a point where you pay real money for a videogame copy, but that videogame is online only, meaning you can't play it offline, or if your internet goes down for example, and not only that, the developers of said game, can, at any time shut down the servers of the game, making it completely unplayable. A game you payed money for, a game that alot of videogame development companies have come to agree that you "rent", you do not buy it, and that is absurd. We as the gaming industry as a whole, we want a game we payed real life money for, to be accessible to us players, even when the developers decide to end support on the game, so maybe the community can continue supporting it I really hope a change can be made, thank you.
